meta_title: Impact Doors in Cypress Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Cypress Lakes, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors in Cypress Lakes, FL — Built to Guard What Matters Most
intro:
Cypress Lakes is one of Palm Beach County's most comfortable and established residential communities — a place where families have planted roots, built equity, and created real lives around golf courses, quiet streets, and a strong neighborhood identity. With average home values sitting around $310,000, that investment deserves serious protection, and in South Florida, serious protection starts at the front door. Impact doors aren't an upgrade — they're a necessity. Whether you're in the heart of the Cypress Lakes Community or on a quiet cul-de-sac close to the Cypress Lakes Golf Course, your home faces wind threats that demand more than a standard door and a set of shutters.

Cypress Lakes sits approximately 10 miles inland from the Atlantic coastline in the 33409 ZIP code, placing it just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — but don't let that distinction give you a false sense of security. The area is rated for 140 mph wind speeds under Florida Building Code, meaning every exterior door on your home must meet stringent hurricane-rated product standards. When a powerful storm tracks across Palm Beach County, there's no inland buffer zone that stops wind-driven rain, flying debris, or the pressure differentials that can blow an ordinary door off its hinges. Cypress Lakes has seen that firsthand through hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma — storms that left lasting impressions on homes throughout the area.

About 45% of homes in Cypress Lakes were built before 1994, which means nearly half the neighborhood's housing stock predates Florida's modern hurricane building code standards. Older entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors installed during that era were never designed to handle today's code-required wind loads or missile impact ratings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — specializes in replacing these aging, vulnerable doors with modern, code-compliant impact door systems from trusted brands ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. From heavy-duty entry doors to wide-panel sliding glass doors that open onto your patio, we bring Cypress Lakes homes up to the standard they deserve.

section_why.h2: Why Cypress Lakes Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact door company isn't just about picking a product off a shelf — it's about trusting the team that pulls your permit, measures your openings, handles your installation, and stands behind the work when you need them. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on a simple philosophy: do the work right, do it in-house, and treat every homeowner like a neighbor. That philosophy resonates deeply in close-knit communities like Cypress Lakes, where word travels fast and quality workmanship gets noticed.

With over 60 years of combined installation experience and more than 500 homes completed across South Florida, our team has encountered virtually every door configuration, structural challenge, and code scenario that Palm Beach County can throw at us. We don't subcontract our labor — every measurement, removal, installation, and finish work is handled by our own trained, licensed, and insured technicians. That means consistent quality from the first estimate to the final inspection, with no handoffs to third parties who may cut corners or lack local code knowledge specific to the Palm Beach County Building Division.

Our process begins with a thorough in-home assessment where we evaluate your existing door frames, identify any structural reinforcement needs, and walk you through your options for impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors. We handle the permitting process with Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf, coordinate the installation around your schedule, and ensure every product passes final inspection before we consider the job complete. For Cypress Lakes homeowners — many of whom have lived in their homes for decades — this level of accountability and professionalism makes all the difference.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing also carries an A+ BBB rating, which reflects our commitment to transparent pricing, honest timelines, and responsive customer service. We're not a high-pressure sales operation. We're a team of specialists who believe Cypress Lakes families deserve to feel safe in their homes year-round, and we back that belief with products, craftsmanship, and service that earn long-term trust.

section_history.h2: Cypress Lakes Has Faced the Storm — Here's What the History Teaches Us
section_history.body:
Palm Beach County has been tested by at least seven named storms since 1990, and Cypress Lakes has been in the path of some of the most consequential. Hurricane Frances made landfall in September 2004, crossing Florida as a Category 2 and hammering the Palm Beach area with sustained winds and torrential rainfall for hours on end. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma arrived in October 2005 — one of the fastest-moving and most intense storms to cross South Florida in recorded history. Wilma's rapid forward speed generated extreme wind shear that caught many homeowners off guard, shattering windows, tearing off roof sections, and blowing compromised doors completely inward. The combination of Frances and Wilma in back-to-back seasons left a lasting mark on the Cypress Lakes community and accelerated awareness of the need for impact-rated products.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 served as a more recent reminder. Though Irma's worst impacts were felt further south and along the Gulf Coast, Palm Beach County experienced significant tropical-force and near-hurricane-force winds that exposed vulnerabilities in homes that hadn't been updated since the pre-code era. Sliding glass doors — often the weakest point in a home's exterior envelope — proved particularly susceptible to pressure failures and frame racking. For the 45% of Cypress Lakes homes built before 1994, Irma was a wake-up call that older doors were no longer adequate for the storm environment South Florida has come to expect.

These storms don't just test products — they reveal the difference between homes that were prepared and homes that weren't. Impact doors aren't just about surviving the wind; they protect against water intrusion, maintain structural integrity under pressure, and provide a critical barrier against airborne debris that can turn ordinary objects into projectiles during a major storm. For Cypress Lakes homeowners still relying on standard doors or removable shutters, each hurricane season represents an unnecessary gamble.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Palm Beach County falls under the Florida Building Code's requirements for high-wind regions, and Cypress Lakes carries a design wind speed rating of 140 mph. While the community is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — this does not reduce the product performance requirements that apply to your home. All exterior door replacements in Palm Beach County must use hurricane-rated products that have been tested and approved in accordance with Florida Building Code Chapter 16 wind load requirements and ASTM E1886/E1996 impact standards. Standard doors, regardless of their aesthetic quality, do not qualify.

Every impact door installation in Cypress Lakes requires a permit issued through the Palm Beach County Building Division. This isn't a bureaucratic hurdle — it's a consumer protection mechanism that ensures your installation is reviewed by a licensed inspector who verifies code compliance, proper anchoring, and correct product specification. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we manage the entire permit process for you. We prepare and submit the documentation, schedule the inspection, and coordinate our crew's schedule around inspection timelines so your project moves forward without delays. Homeowners who attempt to skip permits on impact door installations risk voiding their product warranties, facing fines, and encountering complications when they sell their homes or file insurance claims.

For pre-1994 homes in Cypress Lakes, the permitting process may also involve a structural review of existing door frames and rough openings to ensure the installation can be anchored to current code standards. Our technicians are experienced in identifying and addressing these older framing conditions, recommending reinforcement where necessary and documenting all work for the inspecting authority. When the job is done, you'll have a permitted, inspected, and code-compliant impact door system that satisfies both the Palm Beach County Building Division and your insurance carrier.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Purpose-Built for Cypress Lakes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact door systems from two of the industry's most respected manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered and tested specifically for Florida's demanding storm environment, and both carry the product approvals required for installation in Palm Beach County under the Florida Building Code. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door, upgrading a set of French doors that open to your backyard, or installing new sliding glass doors that face the prevailing storm wind direction, ESW and CWI have configurations designed to perform at the highest level.

ESW impact entry doors are built with reinforced steel or fiberglass door slabs that are resistant to both wind pressure and debris impact per ASTM E1996 large-missile testing standards. Their multi-point locking systems engage the door frame at multiple points simultaneously — not just at the handle — creating a uniform seal that standard single-point locks simply cannot match. This is especially important during storms, where pressure differences between the inside and outside of your home can create enormous force against the door slab and frame. ESW's systems are also engineered for long-term Florida use, with finishes and hardware that resist the corrosion and UV degradation that South Florida's climate accelerates.

CWI's impact door line offers Cypress Lakes homeowners exceptional flexibility in design, including wide-format sliding glass door panels that are ideal for homes with pool decks or screened lanais. CWI sliding glass doors feature impact-laminated glass that holds together upon impact rather than shattering into dangerous shards, combined with heavy-duty aluminum frames and multi-point locking systems that keep the panel sealed and secure through sustained wind events. French door configurations from CWI bring an architectural elegance to both front and rear entries while meeting all 140 mph wind zone requirements applicable to the 33409 area. Both brands come with manufacturer warranties that cover glass, hardware, and frame integrity.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Cypress Lakes Homeowners
section_cost.body:
Understanding the investment involved in impact door installation helps Cypress Lakes homeowners make confident, informed decisions — and the good news is that the numbers make sense both short-term and long-term. For a typical home in the 33409 ZIP code with an average value around $310,000, a single impact entry door installation generally ranges from $1,800 to $3,500 depending on door size, material (fiberglass vs. steel), glass configuration, and hardware selections. Sliding glass door systems, which span larger openings and involve more glass surface area, typically range from $2,500 to $6,000 per unit. French door pairs fall in a similar range depending on panel width and design complexity. Full-home door replacement projects combining entry, sliding, and French doors can range considerably based on scope, but most Cypress Lakes homes see complete projects completed in the $6,000 to $15,000 range.

For homeowners who prefer not to pay the full amount upfront, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified customers. This makes upgrading your entire door system accessible without depleting emergency savings or equity — particularly important for middle-income households in Cypress Lakes where budgeting matters. Our financing programs are designed to keep monthly payments manageable while letting you move forward on protection that your home needs now, not after the next storm season begins.

The return on investment for impact doors in Palm Beach County is compelling. Beyond the immediate safety and security benefit, impact doors contribute measurably to your home's resale value — buyers in South Florida actively seek impact-rated products and pay premiums for homes where the work is already done and permitted. Energy efficiency gains from impact-rated door glass and tight multi-point seals also reduce cooling costs year-round in Cypress Lakes's hot climate. And when you factor in the insurance premium savings that impact doors unlock — which we detail below — the total financial picture becomes even more attractive.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings on Impact Doors for Cypress Lakes Residents
section_insurance.body:
One of the most tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in Cypress Lakes is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums — and in today's Florida insurance market, where carriers are raising rates and reducing coverage availability, those savings carry real weight. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts to homeowners who install wind-mitigation features, including impact-resistant doors and windows. The specific discount percentage varies by carrier and policy structure, but Palm Beach County homeowners with fully impact-rated openings — meaning all exterior windows and doors carry approved impact ratings — often see annual premium reductions in the range of 15% to 45% depending on their home's construction year, roof type, and overall wind mitigation score.

The savings mechanism works through the Florida Wind Mitigation Inspection Report, which is completed by a licensed inspector who verifies that your openings meet the required standards. When your report reflects impact-rated doors and windows on all exterior openings, it triggers the highest available opening protection credit on your policy. For a home in Cypress Lakes carrying a homeowner's insurance premium of $4,000 to $6,000 annually — not uncommon in Palm Beach County's current market — a 25% discount represents $1,000 to $1,500 per year in savings. Over a 10-year period, those savings can offset a significant portion of your original door installation investment.

Common carriers operating in the Palm Beach County market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and Heritage Property & Casualty — all recognize the wind mitigation credits that impact doors generate. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides full documentation of your installed products, including the Notice of Acceptance (NOA) numbers and product approval documentation that your insurance carrier and wind mitigation inspector will need to apply the maximum available credits. We also recommend that Cypress Lakes homeowners schedule a wind mitigation re-inspection promptly after installation to begin capturing savings at the next policy renewal.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Cypress Lakes if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Cypress Lakes is rated for 140 mph design wind speeds under the Florida Building Code, regardless of its inland location. Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma all caused significant wind damage in the area. Being 10 miles from the coast provides no meaningful protection from sustained hurricane-force winds or flying debris. Impact doors are required for code-compliant door replacements in Palm Beach County, and they're critical to protecting your home and family during any major storm event.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost, and what financing does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offer?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Cypress Lakes typically range from $1,800 to $3,500 for a single entry door and $2,500 to $6,000 for sliding glass door systems, depending on size, material, and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. We provide free in-home estimates with transparent, itemized pricing so you know exactly what you're paying for before any work begins.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact doors in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Palm Beach County require a permit through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application and documentation to scheduling the required inspection. Proper permitting protects your warranty, ensures code compliance, and is required by most insurance carriers. Unpermitted impact door installations can create serious problems when you sell your home or file a storm damage claim.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Cypress Lakes?
faqs.a: Most single impact door installations are completed in one day. Full-home projects involving multiple entry, sliding glass, and French door replacements typically take two to three days depending on scope and any structural modifications required for older frames. Permit processing through the Palm Beach County Building Division generally takes one to three weeks, which we initiate before scheduling your installation. We coordinate all inspections and keep you informed throughout the entire process so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance in Cypress Lakes?
faqs.a: Yes — often significantly. Florida law mandates that insurance carriers offer discounts for wind mitigation features, and impact-rated doors on all exterior openings qualify for the highest opening protection credit available. Palm Beach County homeowners with fully impact-rated openings commonly see annual premium reductions of 15% to 45% depending on their carrier and home characteristics. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all necessary product documentation for your wind mitigation inspection, helping you maximize insurance savings at your next policy renewal.

cta_headline: Protect Your Cypress Lakes Home — Get Your Free Impact Door Estimate Today
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to assess your home, answer your questions, and deliver the impact door installation that Cypress Lakes deserves. Contact The Kings of Service today and let's get your home protected before the next storm season arrives.
